title: rosa lutea var. laeta
intro: Rosa lutea var. laeta, commonly known as Golden Prairie Rose, is a species of rose native to the prairies of North America. This plant is valued for its potential health benefits, which include anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, helping to protect against cell damage and reduce inflammation. The therapeutic actions of Rosa lutea var. laeta are attributed to its bioactive constituents, including flavonoids and phenolic acids, which have been shown to exhibit antimicrobial and anti-cancer properties. However, I couldn't find more detailed information on the specific bioactive constituents of Rosa lutea var. laeta.
benefits: Rosa lutea var. laeta has many benefits, such as promoting overall health and well-being, reducing inflammation and relieving pain, improving digestion and boosting the immune system, and even supporting mental health by reducing anxiety and stress. This plant's antiseptic properties can also aid in wound healing, while its antioxidant properties can help protect against cell damage and oxidative stress. Additionally, Rosa lutea var. laeta has been known to have anti-inflammatory properties, which can help alleviate symptoms of conditions such as arthritis and other inflammatory diseases, ultimately improving the quality of life for those who use it. By incorporating Rosa lutea var. laeta into their daily routine, individuals can experience a range of benefits that can lead to improved physical and mental health, increased energy, and a more balanced and fulfilling life.

constituents: Rosa lutea var. laeta has many active constituents, such as flavonoids (quercetin, kaempferol, and myricetin), phenolic acids (ferulic acid and sinapic acid), and volatile oils (geraniol and linalool), which have been found to possess anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties. These constituents contribute to the plant's ability to alleviate symptoms of various health conditions, including reducing inflammation and improving cardiovascular health. The flavonoids, in particular, have been shown to improve memory and cognitive function, while the phenolic acids have been found to have antidiabetic properties. side_effects: Rosa lutea var. laeta can have side effects if used improperly, such as allergic reactions, skin irritation, and digestive issues. Ingesting the plant can cause nausea, vomiting, and diarrhea due to its potential toxicity. Prolonged exposure to the plant's essential oils may also lead to headaches, dizziness, and respiratory problems.
